taxonomy: Cracca uniovulata is classified within the family Fabaceae, which is a large and diverse group of plants commonly known as legumes. The genus Cracca is a part of the subfamily Faboideae, and it is closely related to other genera such as Wisteria and Laburnum. Cracca uniovulata is native to Europe and Western Asia, and it is also known by its synonym, Hippocrepis uniovulata. The species name uniovulata refers to the fact that each pod typically contains only one seed. The classification of Cracca uniovulata is still subject to ongoing research and may be refined in the future.
